what_it_does: Records a design critique warning that proposed extension slots, a radial menu, and aspirational future modes in a brief carry hidden costs and risk becoming committed scope.
observations: Item is linked as part_of board://item-eb5d71e7
Authored by ch-3mpkjg3r and filed on 2026-06-24
Title frames the entry as point 7 of a larger set (P9 · 7)
Challenges Tim's repeated advice to 'leave empty slots for later'
Argues that empty extension slots and mode generalizations are a cost, not a freebie
Criticizes the radial menu proposal (BD-G) as poor for one-handed phone use and accessibility-hostile
Lists specific aspirational additions (image annotation, voice notes, pinch-to-resize, lasso multi-select, breadcrumbs, collapse/expand) and warns each is a real sub-project
References brief items by shorthand codes (BD-A, BD-B, BD-G, BD-K, BD-H, MSG2) without linking them
standouts: Explicit skeptic's flag against framework-first abstraction before any concrete end-to-end path works
Direct UX/accessibility critique of a radial menu in a one-handed phone context
Warning that 'additions Tim would appreciate' will be read by eager builders as scope
conventions: Follows the noticeboard item YAML schema (id, address, type, source, state, title, author_session, channel, links, timestamps, history)
Uses a markdown heading that matches the title
Uses bold emphasis and bullet lists for argument structure
Cites external brief items by internal code shorthand
concerns: Tone is persuasive rather than neutral ('Don't build the radial because it sounds cool')
Cites external brief codes without context, so the file is not self-contained
Radial-menu critique states a UX claim without supporting evidence or citations
Assumes an 'eager builder' interpretation without showing that the team has actually read it that way
notes: A cautionary noticeboard entry arguing that speculative extension points and future-mode lists in a design brief are costly scope magnets, with a specific warning against the proposed radial menu.
questions: Which brief items do BD-A, BD-B, BD-G, BD-K, BD-H, and MSG2 refer to?
What is the parent item board://item-eb5d71e7?
Who is Tim and what is his role?
What product or platform is being designed (mobile app, desktop, etc.)?
Is the radial menu already committed or merely proposed?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to flag an over-engineering tax, and the file does exactly that; purpose matches content.
apparent_themes: Hidden cost of extensibility and empty slots
Scope-creep risk from aspirational feature lists
One-handed mobile UX and accessibility skepticism
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to push the team away from speculative generalizations and toward simpler, proven one-handed mobile interactions before committing to a radial menu or multi-mode framework.
novelty: Distinct as a deliberately contrarian or skeptic's flag inside a design ledger, naming specific brief codes and translating aspirational 'additions Tim would appreciate' into concrete scope warnings.
contribution: Captures a risk perspective that speculative extension slots and future-mode lists can be misread as committed scope, and that a radial menu may be unsuitable for one-handed phone use; a fused system would keep this as a counter-argument record tied to the referenced brief items.
summary_for_embedding: Noticeboard item item-0d8c3efd critiques an over-engineering tax in a design brief, warning that empty extension slots, a radial menu, and listed future modes carry hidden scope and usability costs.
intention_for_embedding: To flag the risk that speculative design abstractions and aspirational feature lists will be treated as committed work, and to argue for simpler concrete interactions first.
